verses.text: Os três filhos de Levi são mencionados, mas suas genealogias completas são apresentadas após a lista dos sumos sacerdotes.

Após o Exílio, a comunidade da Judeia, para quem Crônicas foi escrito, precisava estabelecer inequivocamente a ascendência legítima dos sacerdotes. Esta genealogia de Coate estabeleceu a continuidade entre os ancestrais conhecidos do Pentateuco e os sumos sacerdotes posteriores até o exílio de Judá em 586 a.C.; outros registros conectaram os sacerdotes após o Exílio com esta linhagem (veja **Ed 3.2** ; **7.1-6** ; **Ag 1.1** ). Nem todos os que serviram como sumo sacerdote foram incluídos nesta lista.\n\n

A maioria dos estudiosos do Antigo Testamento concorda que a frase "o sacerdote no Templo que Salomão construiu em Jerusalém" pertence a **6.9** (conectado ao primeiro Azarias), mas que a ordem foi transposta por um erro de escriba. Fazer essa correção resulta em 12 gerações entre Arão e a construção do Templo, o que está de acordo com a data em **1Rs 6.1** (480 anos é o equivalente a 12 gerações de 40 anos cada na tipologia numérica). A correção também resulta em 12 gerações entre a construção do Templo sob Azarias e a reconstrução do Templo sob Josué ( **Ed 3.2** ).

6:11 Azariah begat Amariah, and Amariah begat Ahitub: A central link in the Aaronic high-priestly succession. Ahitub will father Zadok (6:12), the priest who served David and Solomon and whose line would dominate the post-exilic priesthood (cp. 2 Sam 8:17; Ezek 44:15).

6:12 Ahitub begat Zadok, and Zadok begat Shallum: Zadok's loyalty to David secured his line's permanent place in the priesthood. The Chronicler traces it forward through Shallum, anchoring the post-exilic priesthood in the Davidic-Solomonic origin (cp. 1 Kgs 1:8, 39; 2:35).

6:13 Shallum begat Hilkiah, and Hilkiah begat Azariah: Hilkiah is most likely the high priest who discovered the Book of the Law during Josiah's renovation of the temple (2 Kgs 22:8), the act that sparked the reform of 622 BC. The line moves from Davidic origin toward the brink of exile.

6:14 Azariah begat Seraiah—He filled the supreme pontifical office at the destruction of Jerusalem, and, along with his deputy and others, he was executed by Nebuchadnezzar's orders at Riblah (Kg2 25:18, Kg2 25:21). The line of high priests, under the first temple, which from Zadok amounted to twelve, terminated with him.

6:15 Jehozadak went into captivity, when the Lord carried away Judah: The high-priestly line meets the exile. Jehozadak's son Jeshua will return with Zerubbabel to lead the rebuilt altar's first sacrifices (Ezra 3:2; Hag 1:1). The priesthood survives the breach, carrying covenant continuity through the rupture.

A segunda introdução dos levitas ( **6.16-19a** ) apresenta os chefes de todas as famílias levíticas ( **6.19b-30** ). A lista contém as genealogias de Gérson ( **6.20-21** ), Coate ( **6.22-24** ) e Merari ( **6.29-30** ).\n\n

Quando serviu sob Eli, Samuel assumiu uma função levítica ( **1Sm 3.1** ).

6:28 the sons of Samuel—The sons of Samuel are here named Vashni and Abiah. The first-born is called Joel (1 Sam 8:2); and this name is given to him in 1 Chr 6:33. It is now generally thought by the best critics that, through an error of the copyists, an omission has been made of the oldest son's name, and that Vashni, which is not the name of a person, merely signifies "and the second." This critical emendation of the text makes all clear, as well as consistent with other passages relating to the family of Samuel.

6:29 And these are they that waited with their children,.... They and their posterity, who officiated in the service of singing psalms in the sanctuary: the three heads of them were of the three families of the Levites, as follow: of the sons of the Kohathites, Heman a singer; the chief of the singers, and who composed psalms and hymns, which are in the book of Psalms: the son of Joel, the son of Shemuel; or Samuel. This Heman was grandson of Samuel the prophet;

6:30 Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, Asaiah his son: Three generations of the Merarite line, the Levitical branch responsible for the tabernacle's frames and bases (Num 3:36-37). Their service was structural rather than ceremonial — quiet, indispensable work in the economy of worship.
Esta genealogia nomeia os principais cantores de Coate ( **6.33** ), Gérson ( **6.39** ) e Merari ( **6.44** ), as principais famílias levíticas.\n\n
Esta genealogia nomeia os principais cantores de Coate ( **6.33** ), Gérson ( **6.39** ) e Merari ( **6.44** ), as principais famílias levíticas.\n\n
Hemã ocupava a posição de liderança entre os cantores de Davi (veja **Sl 88: título** ).

6:34 The son of Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, the son of Toah: Four ascending generations toward Heman (6:33), David's chief singer. The reverse-order genealogy traces the family back from Heman to Levi (cp. 6:38), establishing the Levitical credential for temple song.

6:35 The son of Zuph, the son of Elkanah, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai: Continuing Heman's ascending line. *Zuph* connects this family to the territory of Zuphim, where Saul searched for his lost donkeys and encountered Samuel (1 Sam 9:5) — the Levitical singer Heman and the prophet Samuel share an ancestral district.

6:36 The son of Elkanah, the son of Joel, the son of Azariah, the son of Zephaniah: Four more generations. The name *Elkanah* recurs in the line — by tradition Hannah's husband (1 Sam 1:1) belongs to this very family, making Heman the singer a descendant of the prophet Samuel.

6:37 The son of Tahath, the son of Assir, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah: The line ascends to Korah, leader of the rebellion against Moses (Num 16). That the Korahites later became one of the great families of temple song (cp. Pss 42–49 titles) shows judgment did not annul mercy — Korah's grandchildren served at the altar.

6:38 The son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel: The ascending genealogy reaches its summit — Levi son of Israel. Heman the singer (6:33) is grafted into the patriarchal line through fifteen generations, his ministry of song a Levitical inheritance reaching back to Sinai itself.
Asafe escreveu vários salmos ( **Sl 50** ; **73-83** ).

6:40 The son of Michael, the son of Baaseiah, the son of Malchiah: A second singer-line, that of Asaph (6:39), is now traced. Asaph's psalms (Pss 50, 73–83) bore his name into later worship; the genealogy here legitimates his standing in the Levitical brotherhood.

6:41 The son of Ethni, the son of Zerah, the son of Adaiah: Continued ascent in Asaph's line. The names — most appearing nowhere else in Scripture — are preserved not for narrative interest but for priestly registry: covenant memory does not forget the obscure servants.

6:42 The son of Ethan, the son of Zimmah, the son of Shimei: A link in the Gershonite Levitical line ascending toward Asaph (6:39-43). The Ethan here is distinct from the Merarite Ethan of 6:44, though the name is shared. Tribal genealogies preserved overlapping names without confusion within their communities.

6:43 The son of Jahath, the son of Gershom, the son of Levi: Asaph's line reaches Gershom, eldest son of Levi (cp. 23:7). Where Heman traced through Kohath (6:38), Asaph traces through Gershom — the temple choir drew from multiple Levitical houses, the music of worship a genuinely shared inheritance.

6:44 Now these are their dwelling places,.... The dwelling places of the priests and Levites, assigned and given to them in the several tribes of Israel; and the account of them agrees with that in Jos 21:1 with some few variations of names of places, which have been there observed: see the notes there; only in 1 Chr 6:57 two cities are omitted, Juttah and Gibeon, through want of care in transcribing, since they are said to be "thirteen", as they should be, whereas eleven only are mentioned.

6:45 The son of Hashabiah, the son of Amaziah, the son of Hilkiah: The third singer-line, that of Ethan the Merarite (6:44), is now traced. Three families — Heman, Asaph, Ethan — together led David's musical service (cp. 15:17, 19), each grounded in one of Levi's three principal houses.

6:46 The son of Amzi, the son of Bani, the son of Shamer: Ethan's line ascends through more generations. The triple-line structure of the singers (Heman-Asaph-Ethan) modeled the unity of Levi's tribe in worship — three families, three branches, one ministry.

6:47 The son of Mahli, the son of Mushi, the son of Merari, the son of Levi: Ethan's line reaches Merari, Levi's third son. The three great singer-genealogies (Heman through Kohath, Asaph through Gershom, Ethan through Merari) together gather all of Levi into the temple's worship — no branch excluded from praise.

6:48 Their brethren also the Levites were appointed unto all manner of service—Those of them who were endowed with musical tastes and talents were employed in various other departments of the temple service.
Esta explicação sobre as prerrogativas dos sacerdotes introduz a lista a seguir.

A lista de sacerdotes de Arão a Aimaás serve como introdução à lista territorial ( **6.54-81** ). A lista vai até o tempo de Davi, quando Aimaás serviu ( **2Sm 15.36** ; **17.17-29** ; **18.19-29** ).

A lista das cidades levíticas é derivada de **Js 21** , seguindo uma lógica geográfica, com famílias agrupadas em distritos geográficos definidos. Para mostrar a unidade e a completude de Israel, a lista territorial apresenta os levitas estabelecidos na terra na mesma medida que as outras tribos.\n\n
